summary: Matched and linked the ittarucann item translation unit by defining its data/table objects in C, cleaning up local symbol names, and resolving the last small codegen mismatches in two functions. configure.py changed ittarucann.c from NonMatching to Matching. decomp.dev reported 5 new GALE01 matches: ittarucann .data, .rodata, .sdata2, it_3F14_Logic5_Spawned, and it_802962E0.
what_changed: Added includes for types.h and baselib/forward.h in ittarucann.c.
Replaced extern declarations for it_803B8610 and it_803B8634 with C definitions: a const lbColl_80008D30_arg1 initializer and a const Vec3 { 0.0f, 1.0f, 0.0f }.

Defined ItemStateTable it_803F63C0[] in C with 10 motion table entries using itTarucann_UnkMotion*_Anim/Phys/Coll callbacks, NULL for one collision callback, and HSD_GObjEvent casts for itTarucann_UnkMotion7_Phys entries.
Added static f32 fake1() and fake2() helpers returning -1.0f and 1.3089969f; the diff gives no explanatory comment for these beyond their presence.
Adjusted it_3F14_Logic5_Spawned by introducing temp_var and rewriting the random angle addition as (temp_var = 1.3089969f) + temp, next to an existing TODO noting a float register-swap issue.
Adjusted it_802962E0 absolute-value logic from local temp plus if-statement to a ternary expression that re-reads ip->xDD4_itemVar.tarucann.x10.
Changed configure.py so melee/it/items/ittarucann.c is built as Object(Matching, ...) instead of Object(NonMatching, ...).
Changed symbols.txt entries for several data, sdata, and sdata2 literals from global it_ address labels to local @NNN labels, including @314, @312, @313, and @188 through @350-range constants.
smart_moves: Moved item data out of extern-only declarations and into exact C initializers, allowing .data and related sections for the item unit to match and link.
Used a non-const ItemStateTable definition for it_803F63C0, matching its .data placement rather than forcing it into a const section.
Preserved table layout details, including the NULL callback slot and explicit HSD_GObjEvent casts where callback types did not naturally line up.

Solved small function-level mismatches with source expression rewrites rather than assembly, especially the assignment-expression float constant in it_3F14_Logic5_Spawned and ternary absolute-value form in it_802962E0.
Cleaned symbol scope by demoting compiler-local strings/floats/doubles from global it_ labels to @NNN local labels once the C translation unit owned the data.
Promoted the object to Matching in configure.py in the same PR that made the supporting symbol and source changes.
decomp_lessons: When matching item units, define article constants and state tables in the item C file instead of leaving them as externs if the symbols are part of the same object.
For ItemStateTable reconstruction, exact callback order, motion IDs, NULL entries, and casts can matter for matching the .data bytes.
If a table symbol lives in .data, avoid adding const unless the original object really placed it in a read-only section.

Last-mile float mismatches may require expression-shape changes that influence register allocation or constant materialization; the PR's TODO explicitly calls this a float regswap in it_3F14_Logic5_Spawned.
Ternary expressions can produce different codegen from assigning to a temporary and branching, even when the behavior is the same.
Once data is matched from C, update symbols.txt to represent compiler-generated local literals as @NNN locals rather than global address-style names.

Unused static helper functions named fake1/fake2 appear to be matching scaffolding for constants, but the slice has no review/body explanation, so future work should verify before copying that tactic.
naming_conventions: Item callbacks in this file use the itTarucann_ prefix with UnkMotionN_Anim, UnkMotionN_Phys, and UnkMotionN_Coll suffixes.
Address-style names such as it_803B8610, it_803B8634, it_803F63C0, it_3F14_Logic5_Spawned, and it_802962E0 remain where semantic names are not established.
Compiler-local literals in symbols.txt use @NNN names and local scope, while true global data retains it_ address labels.
The added fake1/fake2 names should be treated as non-semantic matching scaffolding, not as item-domain terminology.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Exact data initializers matched previously unmatched ittarucann .data and related constants.
ItemStateTable it_803F63C0[] was reconstructed manually with 10 entries and precise callback/cast layout.
The expression `(temp_var = 1.3089969f) + temp` was used to resolve a float register/order mismatch in it_3F14_Logic5_Spawned.
The absolute-value check in it_802962E0 was rewritten as `field < 0.0f ? -field : field` instead of assigning the field to a temp first.
Static f32 fake1/fake2 returning constants were added; likely a constant-emission/order tactic, but this is weakly evidenced because the PR has no explanatory body or review.
symbols.txt was updated so local data/string/float/double literals became @314, @312, @313, @188, @189, @190, @191, @192, @205, @207, @210, @211, @227, @229, @276, @277, @278, and @350.
review_feedback: No human review comments and no PR body were present in the slice.

decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 improvements: matched code 54.54% (+0.02%, +652 bytes), linked code 31.30% (+0.17%, +6664 bytes), matched data 34.55% (+0.03%, +320 bytes), and linked data 28.92% (+0.03%, +336 bytes).
Bot listed 5 new matches for main/melee/it/items/ittarucann: .data +200 to 100.00%, .rodata +48 to 100.00%, .sdata2 +5 to 100.00%, it_3F14_Logic5_Spawned +2 to 100.00%, and it_802962E0 +2 to 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Search for other item files with TODO float regswap and compare whether assignment-expression constants solve the same pattern.
Inspect it_3F14_Logic2_Spawned because the diff comment says the float regswap is the same as in that function.
Audit whether fake1/fake2 in ittarucann.c are still required for constant placement after later matching work.
Compare ItemStateTable definitions in neighboring item files for HSD_GObjEvent cast conventions on Phys callbacks.
If ittarucann sdata2 regresses, check symbols around 0x804DCB78 through 0x804DCBBC and local labels @188 through @350.
